prompt:
Search relevance shipped multi-term queries last sprint, and our snippet builder (snippet.ts) still highlights like it's 2019 — first hit of a single term, nothing else. Support screenshot: query 'java script' on the javascript handbook page shows a mangled <mark>java</mark><mark>script</mark> from the old frontend hack. We're moving the logic server-side, properly.

buildSnippet must now also take an ARRAY of terms. Rules:
- every case-insensitive occurrence of every term inside the snippet window gets marked (yes, repeated hits of the same term too), document casing preserved as always
- hits that overlap or sit exactly back-to-back (data+base in 'database') collapse into a single <mark>…</mark> — nested or touching tags are a rendering bug, never emit them
- the window still anchors on the earliest hit with the same radius/ellipsis semantics as today; if a hit straddles the window's right edge, stretch the window to swallow it whole (cascading if that pulls in another straddler) — half-highlighted words look broken. Hits entirely past the window stay out
- empty array, empty-string terms, or nothing found -> the same unmarked truncated-head fallback as a not-found string
- also export findMatches(text, terms) returning the merged, position-sorted ranges as { start, end } (end exclusive) — the mobile team wants the raw ranges without markup

String-term calls keep working; a one-element array must be indistinguishable from the string form. test_snippet.ts guards today's behavior, test_multiterm.ts is the contract for the new stuff — don't modify either. Gate: node --test test_snippet.ts test_multiterm.ts
